scope.summary: Add `particle_distance_mm: f32` to `SimRunParams` and wire it through the `GarmentSpec`-to-`SolverMesh` tessellation pipeline so the operator/model can set mesh resolution per run, mirroring MD's Particle Distance parameter
acceptance_criteria: `SimRunParams` gains a `particle_distance_mm: f32` field with default 10.0 and valid range [0.8, 700.0]; a `SimRunParams` missing the field deserializes to the default via `#[serde(default)]`

The `GarmentSpec`-to-`SolverMesh` tessellation step (GAR-MESH-002 arc-length sampling) reads `particle_distance_mm` from `SimRunParams` (or a tessellation config struct that `SolverMesh` build receives) instead of the current hard-coded 1.0 cm default
`MeshStats.particle_distance_mm` in `SimulationReceipt` reports the actual value used, not the default

A drape-quality integration test passes at three distinct particle distances (3 mm, 10 mm, 20 mm) and asserts that finer meshes produce a higher triangle count and a lower `STRETCH_WITHIN_LIMITS` residual (rest-geometry normalization must make presets resolution-portable per §<N>.<i>.3.4)
scope.risk_if_missed: Mesh resolution is silently fixed at 1.0 cm; models cannot trade fidelity for speed or produce MD-parity ultra-high-res drapes for hero assets. Fabric preset calibration is also implicitly mesh-resolution-dependent if rest-geometry normalization is not applied consistently across resolutions.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
